HEAIRNG RE: PLAINTIFF GARROW GEER's Motion For Protective Order Re Defendant Auroratech Company's Second Set Of Requests For Admission, Nos. 17-138.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for plaintiff (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF GARROW GEER'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED. THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS NOS 17 -138 ARE AN IMPROPER USE OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WHICH ARE TO BE USED TO NARROW THE ISSUES IN DISPUTE. WHEN COMBINED WITH FORM INTERROGATORY 17'1 THESE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION IMPOSE AN UNREASONABLE BURDEN UPON PLAINTIFF. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT KURA'S DECLARATION FOR REQUESTS IN EXCESS OF 35 IS INSUFFICIENT TO SHOW A JUSTIFICATION FOR THIS ADDITION DISCOVERY AS MOST OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS RELATE TO ISSUES WHICH HAVE PREVIOUSLY BEEN CONCLUSIVELY DETERMINED MAKING ANY REQUEST FOR ADMISSION MOOT.
THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTION AGAINST ANY PARTY.

Order signed.
